tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57911,"title":"[Azure AI Projects Agents] Docs: README Toolboxes section is incomplete and install snippet has wrong package name","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57780) by `@nick863` ## What Changed PR #57780 added the `AgentToolboxes` client, `ToolboxRecord`/`ToolboxVersion` models, and a full CRUD sample (`Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md` and `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`) for storing and retrieving agent tools in Azure. The README was partially updated to add a `### Toolboxes` section, but several operations and the client instantiation step were omitted. ## Gaps Found - **Wrong package name in "Install the package" section:** the `dotnet add package` command references `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` instead of `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`. - **Toolboxes section missing client setup:** The README does not show how to obtain an `AgentToolboxes` instance via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()` (snippet `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` exists in the sample file but is absent from the README). - **Toolboxes section missing Update operation:** `UpdateToolbox` / `UpdateToolboxAsync` (snippets `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) are not mentioned in the README. - **Toolboxes section missing List operations:** `GetToolboxVersions` / `GetToolboxVersionsAsync` (snippets `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) and `GetToolboxes` / `GetToolboxesAsync` (snippets `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) are not mentioned. - **Toolboxes section missing Delete operations:** `DeleteToolboxVersion` / `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync` and `DeleteToolbox` / `DeleteToolboxAsync` (snippets `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async`) are not mentioned. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Fix the wrong package name in the "Install the package" section **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **edit** Find: ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease ``` Replace with: ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The test file already has the required snippet regions. The work is to add the corresponding README placeholders so the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool fills them in. **Test file (already exists):** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` Verify the following `#region` names are present in that file (they should be): - `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` - `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` - `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` - `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` - `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Sync` / `_Async` **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **edit** Inside the `### Toolboxes` section, insert the following content (replacing the existing partial section). The updated `### Toolboxes` section should read: ````markdown ### Toolboxes Toolboxes allow us to store tools in Azure so that they can be retrieved and used by the Agents. As for the Hosted Agent, we will need to set the experimental header `Toolboxes=V1Preview`, and we also need to disable the `AAIP001` warning. ```C# #pragma warning disable AAIP001 ``` First, create an `AgentToolboxes` client using `GetAgentToolboxes()` on an `AgentAdministrationClient` configured with the experimental feature header: ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD ``` #### Create toolbox versions ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Retrieve a toolbox record `ToolboxRecord` contains the toolbox name and its default version: ```C# Snippet:Sample_GetToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Retrieve a specific toolbox version ```C# Snippet:Sample_GetToolboxVersion_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Update the default toolbox version ```C# Snippet:Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### List all toolbox versions ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### List all toolboxes ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` #### Delete toolbox versions and toolboxes ```C# Snippet:Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains the required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Tracing, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No structural changes beyond the Toolboxes sub-section are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — verify the README now contains the code extracted from the test file in each placeholder block (check `git diff`) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — no public API surface changed here, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24114381665/agentic_workflow) · ● 452.4K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57911","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T02:32:28Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T02:32:28Z"}

- MCPToolDefinition does not support the project_connection_id property like in Python MCPTool class","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

The class MCPToolDefinition has no way to set the `project_connection_id` property like we can on the [MCPTool class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-ai-agents/azure.ai.agents.models.mcptool?view=azure-python-preview) in python.

The `project_connection_id` is a property that is necessary when creating an agent that uses a MCP tool already created in Foundry nextgen.

Here are some examples in python:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/main/sdk/ai/azure-ai-projects/samples/agents/tools/sample_agent_mcp_with_project_connection.py#L48

```python
    tool = MCPTool(
        server_label="api-specs",
        server_url="https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp",
        require_approval="always",
        project_connection_id=os.environ["MCP_PROJECT_CONNECTION_ID"],
    )
```

I believe the `project_connection_id` is metadata, in that it does not need to be in the MCPToolResource (i.e. per thread run) thus why it could be in the MCPToolDefinition.

### Expected behavior

Be able to have extra dynamic parameters that can be passed to the REST API of Foundry to align with the Foundry nextgen platform.

### Actual behavior

Impossible to set the the property in any shape or form.

### Reproduction Steps

```csharp
new MCPToolDefinition("MCP-Server-Label", "MCP-Remote-URL")
```

MCPToolDefinition does not expose the `project_connection_id` property

Any use of VisualStudioCredentials with the latest Azure.AI.Projects or Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent fails which makes the use of DefaultAzureCredentials difficult in test and development.

CustomKeys credentials are not serialized correctly","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Projects 1.0.0-beta.11

### Describe the bug

When I use the SDK to get CustomKeys connections, the Credentials part is not serialized correctly. The actual response is compatible with the `DeserializeCustomCredential` method.

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/src/Generated/Models/CustomCredential.Serialization.cs#L71

There is no "keys" properties in the response inside the "credentials" object.

The raw response from the api looks like:
```json
{
  "name" : "**omit**",
  "id" : "**omit**",
  "type" : "CustomKeys",
  "target" : "_",
  "isDefault" : false,
  "credentials" : {
    "password" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**",
    "type" : "CustomKeys"
  },
  "metadata" : {
    "baseUrl" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**",
    "username" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**"
  }
}
```

The `DeserializeCustomCredential` tries to read the key `credentials.keys` as a nested object to create the `Keys` property of the `CustomCredential.Credentials.Keys`

Something like that I guess:
```json
{
  // omit
  "credentials": {
    "type": "CustomKeys",
    "keys": {
      "[KEY_NAME]": "[KEY_VALUE]"
    }
  }
  // omit
}
```

### Expected behavior

I expect the custom keys defined as "secret" in Azure Foundry available when using the AI Project SDK in the Credentials property of `CustomCredential`.

### Actual behavior

Only the non secret custom keys are available in the metadata property.
Nothing in the Credentials property.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Create a AI Foundry Project
2. Add a Custom key connection "test"
3. Add at least one secret key
4. use `AiProjectClient.Connections.GetConnectionAsync("test", includeCredentials: true)`
5. Try to access to `ClientResult<ConnectionProperties>.Value.Credentials.Keys`
6. See `null`

### Environment

Windows 10, Dotnet 9

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue documents a documentation gap in the `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` package README — the install snippet references the wrong package name (`Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`), and the Toolboxes section is missing several operations (client setup, Update, List versions, List all, Delete).

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` (prerelease)
**Affected API:** `AgentToolboxes` client — `GetAgentToolboxes()`, `UpdateToolbox`, `GetToolboxVersions`, `GetToolboxes`, `DeleteToolboxVersion`, `DeleteToolbox`
**Scenarios:**
- Wrong `dotnet add package` command in the "Install the package" section (references `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` instead of `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`)
- README Toolboxes section missing client instantiation via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()`
- README Toolboxes section missing Update, List (versions + all toolboxes), and Delete operations despite test file snippets already existing

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` to fix the install snippet and add the missing Toolboxes sub-sections using existing snippet regions from `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no runtime or API surface change is required. The implementation guide in the issue body is detailed and actionable — snippet regions already exist in the test file.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Fix the `dotnet add package` command in the README "Install the package" section (`Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` → `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`).
2. Insert the missing Toolboxes sub-sections into the README using snippet placeholders matching the `#region` names in `Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`.
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` and verify the snippet placeholders are populated correctly via `git diff`.
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` and `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm nothing is broken.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the package `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` is a data-plane client SDK (not management or provisioning)
- **Service:** `AI Projects` — the package namespace (`Azure.AI.Projects.*`) and service directory (`sdk/ai/`) map to the `AI Projects` service label, consistent with similar issues (#56436, #52355)
- **Confidence:** High — the package name is explicit in the issue body, the category and service are unambiguous, and the label selection aligns with quality reference issues
</details>
